headline: McDonald's focus on value and a big new burger drive sales in the first quarter
summary: McDonald’s posted better-than-expected sales in the first quarter as a new burger — and a continuing emphasis on value — brought in customers.  McDonald's shares rose more than 3% before the opening bell Thursday.  The limited-time Big Arch burger —- a 1,020-calorie behemoth that went on sale in the U.S. last month — became a viral sensation after McDonald’s CEO Chris Kempczinski posted a video of himself taking a nibble from one.

content.title: McDonald's focus on value and a big new burger drive sales in the first quarter

headline: Why McDonald's may be a better consumer story than an investment play
summary: McDonald’s (MCD) topped Q1 estimates after four straight quarters of same-store sales growth, but CEO Chris Kempczinski warns business could slow next quarter. Yahoo Finance’s Julie Hyman and Head of News Myles Udland break down why McDonald’s may be a stronger consumer story than an investor story.

content.title: Why McDonald's may be a better consumer story than an investment play
content.description:
McDonald’s (MCD) topped Q1 estimates after four straight quarters of same-store sales growth, but CEO Chris Kempczinski warns business could slow next quarter.

Yahoo Finance’s Julie Hyman and Head of News Myles Udland break down why McDonald’s may be a stronger consumer story than an investor story.
content.summary: McDonald’s (MCD) topped Q1 estimates after four straight quarters of same-store sales growth, but CEO Chris Kempczinski warns business could slow next quarter. Yahoo Finance’s Julie Hyman and Head of News Myles Udland break down why McDonald’s may be a stronger consumer story than an investor story.

headline: McDonald's misses US sales growth target as consumer spending tightens
summary:
STORY: McDonald's missed its U.S. sales growth target in the first quarter.

The world's biggest burger chain posted U.S. same‑store sales growth of 3.9% - below Wall Street estimates.

Thursday's update showed low-price meal deals and limited-time offers struggled to draw diners.

Consumers have recently pulled back on spending due to higher fuel and grocery costs.

CEO Chris Kempczinski said the operating environment was still "challenging."

That was depsite McDonald's beating quarterly estimates for revenue and profit.

Several U.S. restaurant chains like Wingstop and Domino's have reported weaker quarterly sales growth.

They cited a hit to customer spending from soaring gasoline prices caused by the Iran war.

Wall Street analysts also argue lower-income consumers have turned more selective.

It's seen buyers trade down to simpler, single‑item orders rather than full meals.

Data from Placer.ai showed U.S. traffic was uneven at McDonald's through the first quarter.

Same-store visits fell 1.3% in January due to winter storms.

Traffic bounced back just under 4% in February on pent‑up demand, but slipped again in March.

On a global scale, McDonald's found comparable sales rose 3.8% - slightly behind analyst forecasts.

Although it was an improvement on last year's numbers.

The firm's total revenue of $6.52 billion beat estimates.

content.title: McDonald's misses US sales growth target as consumer spending tightens
content.summary:
STORY: McDonald's missed its U.S. sales growth target in the first quarter.

The world's biggest burger chain posted U.S. same‑store sales growth of 3.9% - below Wall Street estimates.

Thursday's update showed low-price meal deals and limited-time offers struggled to draw diners.

Consumers have recently pulled back on spending due to higher fuel and grocery costs.

CEO Chris Kempczinski said the operating environment was still "challenging."

That was depsite McDonald's beating quarterly estimates for revenue and profit.

Several U.S. restaurant chains like Wingstop and Domino's have reported weaker quarterly sales growth.

They cited a hit to customer spending from soaring gasoline prices caused by the Iran war.

Wall Street analysts also argue lower-income consumers have turned more selective.

It's seen buyers trade down to simpler, single‑item orders rather than full meals.

Data from Placer.ai showed U.S. traffic was uneven at McDonald's through the first quarter.

Same-store visits fell 1.3% in January due to winter storms.

Traffic bounced back just under 4% in February on pent‑up demand, but slipped again in March.

On a global scale, McDonald's found comparable sales rose 3.8% - slightly behind analyst forecasts.

Although it was an improvement on last year's numbers.

The firm's total revenue of $6.52 billion beat estimates.

headline: McDonald’s (NYSE:MCD) Q1 CY2026 Sales Beat Estimates
summary: Fast-food chain McDonald’s (NYSE:MCD) reported Q1 CY2026 results topping the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 9.4% year on year to $6.52 billion. Its GAAP profit of $2.78 per share was 1.6% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: McDonald’s (NYSE:MCD) Q1 CY2026 Sales Beat Estimates
content.summary: Fast-food chain McDonald’s (NYSE:MCD) reported Q1 CY2026 results topping the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 9.4% year on year to $6.52 billion. Its GAAP profit of $2.78 per share was 1.6% above analysts’ consensus estimates.
